purpose: Define the minimal experiment-local LGRC-style event ledger needed to represent the D2.3 self-rearming packet pulse mechanism.

time_semantics.scheduler_event_index: Monotone event processing index in the converted ledger.
time_semantics.event_time_key: LGRC-compatible event-time key. For D2.3 conversion this may be derived from the source artifact step surface.
time_semantics.node_proper_time: Node or pole local proper-time surface attached to the event. D2.3 conversion may infer this from event-time when no native LGRC runtime proper-time trace exists.
time_semantics.step_index: Original D2.3 trace step index. This is preserved as source_step_index and is not treated as identical to event_time_key unless marked by inferred_fields.

packet_departure.description: A coherence packet leaves a source pole or source node and enters in-flight packet budget.

packet_arrival.description: A coherence packet arrives at its target pole or target node and leaves in-flight packet budget.

state_trigger.description: A measured pole-state condition crosses the serialized threshold and permits packet departure.

self_rearm.description: A returned packet recreates the source surplus condition that triggers the next departure.

cycle_complete.description: A declared route has completed one ordered closed-loop traversal.

control_blocked.description: A control lane is recorded as blocked or negative for a declared reason.

inference_policy.rule: Any field not directly present in the D2.3 source artifact must be listed in inferred_fields and explained in inference_notes.

route_policy.canonical_route_step: Zero-based index of the channel within the declared route.

semantic_notes.self_rearm: LGRC9V3 can represent the arrival and later departure. The self_rearm label is E1 experiment-level evidence asserting that the later departure was triggered by returned packet coherence.
semantic_notes.state_trigger: LGRC9V3 has packet production surfaces, but D2.3's pole-surplus threshold trigger remains experiment-local in E1.
semantic_notes.control_blocked: Control outcomes are ledger evidence for claim blocking, not runtime packet events.
